text.tspan: Three ways to specialize an LLM for your use case.
Most teams confuse these or pick the wrong one.
The right choice depends on WHAT you're trying to change.
Change how the model responds via prompt:
- System prompt (role, tone, constraints)
- Few-shot examples
- Chain-of-thought / reasoning prompts
Cheap (no training), fast iteration,
but limited - can't really teach new facts
or very different behavior.
Inject facts at query time.
For: domain knowledge, private docs,
changing data, citations.
Doesn't change model behavior - just
what info it has access to.
See ret/basic-rag.md.
Update model weights on your examples.
For: format consistency, style/tone, specialized
task behavior, smaller models matching bigger
models on narrow task.
Doesn't reliably teach facts (use RAG for that).
Expensive, slow iteration, but bakes-in behavior.
LoRA / QLoRA make it cheaper (only some weights updated).
Need access to current/private FACTS? -> RAG
Need consistent FORMAT or BEHAVIOR? -> Fine-tune
Just want better outputs from base model? -> Better prompt
Commonly: combine. Fine-tune for format,
RAG for facts, prompt for orchestration.
Start with prompting; add others as evidence demands.
